name: Neon Manuscript
rationale: Contrasts the illusionist's deceptive perfection against the true source by mapping the scene onto a risograph-printed zine aesthetic — flat neon overlaps and deliberate misregistration make the fake scroll glow with viral wrong-ness while the humble true source reads as warm and grounded.
preamble: Risograph-zine illustration style, circa 2024–2026 indie-print revival. Two-color risograph overprint palette: deep indigo base with fluorescent chartreuse highlights and accidental magenta misregistration halos. Flat, slightly grainy ink textures with visible dot-screen halftone on shadow fills. Line art is sparse and confident — brush-pen weight with zero rendering, no gradients, no glow effects. The forged citation scroll is depicted in chartreuse on indigo, radiating an unsettling hyper-legibility; when dissolved, it reveals blank cream vellum rendered as warm unprinted paper stock. The true source is a dog-eared pamphlet inked purely in the indigo layer — humble, real, tactile. Candlelight is suggested by warm spot-yellow halftone dots bleeding at misregistration. Overall mood: playful academic horror, zine-punk meets illuminated manuscript. Visual references: Perfectly Imperfect Press risograph output, Hato Press zine covers, Studio Tipi two-color print work.
